data: Foot and fragment of wall of a black-glaze kylix.
The text is arranged left-to-right and in scriptio continua on the underside of the foot.

Found in 1968 during the excavations by the Soprintendenza alle Antichità della Sicilia Occidentale in the ancient necropolis in the area of Manico di Quarara, on the slopes of Monte d'Oro di Montelepre, 1.4km north-west of Montelepre.
I.Sicily with the permission of the Assessorato Regionale dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana - Dipartimento dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana
Partially reopened following extensive restoration work. Comprehensive collection of archaeological material, predominantly from the West of the island, with an important Etruscan collection.
Centre of Palermo, Piazza Olivella 24, 90100 - Palermo

Apparatus criticus

  • Text after Agostiniani 1977 (IAS I)

English translation

Italian translation

commentary

Most scholars agree with the division of the text into αταιτυκαι and εμι, in which εμι would have the value of 'I am' and the second term would be an onomastic formula. Agostiniani (1977) hypothesises that this second part of the inscription could be a bimember onomastic formula (αται τυκαι), while Ferri (1968) identifies in the first part of the inscription the name Ατος, attested in Selinunte.
